title: American-Style Veggie Burger (Aloo Tikki Patty)

subs.name: equal parts boiled sweet potato and potato for a sweeter, more colourful patty
2 tbsp rice flour or 1 tbsp cornflour for a lighter, crispier coat
local pav (dinner roll) or wrap it in a phulka for a street-food presentation
green mint-coriander chutney for a fully desi flavour profile

steps.text: Boil the potatoes until completely tender. Drain, peel and mash thoroughly while still warm — lumps will crack the patty during frying.

Mix the mashed potato with the besan, green chilli, cumin seeds, coriander leaves, chopped onion and salt. Knead together for 1–2 minutes until the mixture is uniform and holds its shape when pressed.
Divide into four equal portions. Shape each into a round patty about 1.5 cm thick. Refrigerate for 10 minutes if the mixture feels too soft.

Heat 2 tbsp oil in a flat tawa or frying pan on medium flame. Place two patties and press them gently. Cook without moving for 4–5 minutes until the bottom crust is deep golden and releases cleanly from the pan.

Flip carefully and drizzle another 1 tbsp oil around the edges. Cook for another 4 minutes until the second side is equally golden. The patty should feel firm and not squash when pressed. Repeat with remaining patties.
Toast the cut side of each bun in the residual oil for 30 seconds until golden. Layer: bottom bun, ketchup, a small pile of shredded cabbage, the hot tikki patty and the top bun. Serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: besan → 2 tbsp rice flour or 1 tbsp cornflour for a lighter, crispier coat (rice flour is gluten-free); potato → equal parts boiled sweet potato and potato for a sweeter, more colourful patty; bread → local pav (dinner roll) or wrap it in a phulka for a street-food presentation; tomato-ketchup → green mint-coriander chutney for a fully desi flavour profile
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This recipe is already Indian at heart — the aloo tikki patty is a staple of street stalls from Delhi to Chennai. Use a flat iron tawa and press down lightly to ensure even contact. Serve in pav instead of a burger bun to lean into the Indian format. Add a smear of green chutney for an irresistible Mumbai-style touch.
